commands.review_summary:
The PR adds tart desktop support by advertising `FeatureDesktop`, enabling macOS Screen Sharing for desktop leases, adding a `--tart-user` flag, and documenting native VNC access.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a feature PR; the merge blockers are source-visible in the diff and command routing. The PR body provides live output for the intended native Screen Sharing path, but the unsupported generic paths remain unguarded.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainers need to choose the tart desktop contract and Screen Sharing security boundary before an automated repair lane would be safe.

target.title: feat(tart): desktop/VNC support via guest Screen Sharing
target.body:
## Summary

Follow-up to the tart provider (#209), which shipped headless and deferred desktop. `crabbox warmup --provider tart --desktop` now brings up a visible macOS session by turning on the guest's built-in **Screen Sharing** (native VNC on `127.0.0.1:5900`); connect with a native VNC client over an SSH tunnel.

## How it works

- `FeatureDesktop` is advertised so `--desktop` is accepted.
- During Acquire, for `--desktop` leases the provider enables `com.apple.screensharing` in the guest over `tart exec`. The VM still runs `--no-graphics` — Screen Sharing is a network service and needs no local display.
- Authentication uses the **guest account's own credentials** (macOS user auth). crabbox provisions no separate VNC password, so nothing secret is written to the guest or passed through a command line.

```sh
crabbox warmup --provider tart --desktop
ssh -i <lease-key> -L 5900:127.0.0.1:5900 admin@<lease-ip>
open vnc://127.0.0.1:5900        # macOS Screen Sharing, or any Apple-auth VNC client
```

## Scope (review feedback)

- **`crabbox webvnc` is intentionally not claimed for tart.** Its local bridge (`directSSHWebVNC`) shells a noVNC/`websockify` proxy that is Linux-only (`/usr/share/novnc`, `ss`), so it can't serve a macOS guest. Rather than ship a broken path, this scopes desktop to native Screen Sharing and connects with a native client. A macOS noVNC bridge is a possible follow-up.
- **No credential exposure.** An earlier revision wrote a VNC password and (in one iteration) tried to reset the account password — that failed on cirruslabs' secure-token `admin`, and `tart exec` forwards neither stdin nor env, so any password would land in argv. The final design sidesteps all of it: no managed password, account auth only.

## Live proof — Apple Silicon M4 Pro, tart 2.32.1, macOS 26.4

```
$ crabbox warmup --provider tart --desktop --slug vncn
provisioned lease=cbx_… instance=crabbox-vncn-… state=ready
ready ssh=admin@192.168.64.11:22 …

# RFB handshake to the guest's Screen Sharing server (192.168.64.11:5900):
server banner: RFB 003.889
num security types: 4
offered: [(30,'Apple/Mac-auth'),(33,'Apple-ext'),(36,'Apple-ext3'),(35,'Apple-ext2')]
# -> a real authenticating macOS Screen Sharing server (not an open port)

$ crabbox list --provider tart
crabbox-vncn-…  ready  …  slug=vncn keep=true target=macos
$ crabbox stop --provider tart --id cbx_…
released lease=cbx_… instance=crabbox-vncn-…
# verify: 0 in crabbox list, 0 in tart list (clean teardown)
```

## Files

| File | What |
|------|------|
| `internal/providers/tart/provider.go` | advertise `FeatureDesktop` |
| `internal/providers/tart/backend.go` | enable Screen Sharing on `--desktop` (Acquire) |
| `internal/providers/tart/flags.go` | `--tart-user` |
| `internal/providers/tart/provider_test.go` | tests |
| `docs/providers/tart.md` | Desktop/VNC docs + the noVNC-on-macOS caveat |

## Not included

- noVNC browser bridge for macOS (`crabbox webvnc` on tart).
- Resolution/scaling controls.
